content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- The current node cert expires very soon (Jun 27 2026 - about 2 days from now, June 25 2026)
- The CA cert is valid until 2036
- The CA key and cert are in the openssl-toolbox at /tmp/certs/ca.key and /tmp/certs/ca.crt

I need to:
1. Get the current node.cnf from the toolbox to understand the SAN/subject config
2. Generate a new node certificate signed by the existing CA, valid for ~1 year
3. Also generate a new client.root cert since that's in the secret too
4. Update the Kubernetes secret with the new certs
5. Restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the changes

Let me check what's in the node.cnf file to see the current configuration.

content.text: The current node cert expires in 2 days. I'll now check the existing node config and generate a new 1-year cert.

content.thinking:
Good. Now I have everything I need:
1. The node.cnf is already in the toolbox with all the right SANs
2. The CA key and cert are at /tmp/certs/ca.key and /tmp/certs/ca.crt

My plan:
1. Generate a new node key and CSR in the toolbox using the existing node.cnf
2. Sign it with the CA key (valid for 365 days)
3. Also generate a new client.root cert (since it's also in the secret and may need to be rotated too)
4. Update the Kubernetes secret with new node.crt and node.key
5. Restart the CockroachDB pods

Looking at what's already in the toolbox, I see the node.key is there along with all the other cert files. For the TLS rotation, I'll generate a new CSR using the existing node.key and then sign it with the CA.

Actually, for a proper rotation I should create a fresh key as well. So I'll generate a new node key in the toolbox, create a CSR from it using the node.cnf, sign it with the CA for a year-long validity, then extract everything and update the Kubernetes secret before doing the rolling restart. I should also verify the client.root certificate's expiration while I'm at it.

content.text: The toolbox already has the node.cnf with all the right SANs. I'll generate a new node certificate (and client.root cert) in the toolbox, then update the secret.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.client\.root\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -enddate -subject 2>&1

content.content:
notAfter=Jun 27 09:04:17 2026 GMT
subject=CN = root

content.text: Both certs expire in 2 days. I'll rotate both. Generating new key + CSR for node cert in the toolbox:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key.new 2048 2>&1

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key.new -out /tmp/certs/node.csr.new -config /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1
input.description: Generate new node CSR using existing config

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.crt.new \
  -days 365 \
  -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req \
  -extfile /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1
input.description: Sign new node cert with CA, valid 365 days

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = node

tool_use_result.stdout:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = node

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -noout -enddate -subject -in /tmp/certs/node.crt.new 2>&1

content.content:
notAfter=Jun 25 09:06:58 2027 GMT
subject=CN = node

content.thinking:
New node cert is valid until June 25, 2027 (about 1 year). Now let me generate the client.root cert too.

First, I need to check if there's a client.root.cnf or similar config in the toolbox.

content.text: Node cert good — valid until Jun 25, 2027. Now generating the client.root cert:

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat > /tmp/certs/client.root.cnf << EOF
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no
[req_distinguished_name]
CN = root
[v3_req]
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
EOF'
echo "done"

content.content: done

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new 2048 2>&1 && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl req -new \
  -key /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -config /tmp/certs/client.root.cnf 2>&1 && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new \
  -days 365 \
  -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req \
  -extfile /tmp/certs/client.root.cnf 2>&1
input.description: Generate and sign new client.root cert

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = root

tool_use_result.stdout:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = root

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -noout -enddate -subject -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new 2>&1

content.content:
notAfter=Jun 25 09:07:11 2027 GMT
subject=CN = root

content.text: Both new certs look good. Now I'll extract them from the toolbox and patch the Kubernetes secret:

input.command:
NEW_NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | base64 -w 0)
NEW_NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new | base64 -w 0)
NEW_CLIENT_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new | base64 -w 0)
NEW_CLIENT_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new | base64 -w 0)

echo "NODE_CRT len: ${#NEW_NODE_CRT}"
echo "NODE_KEY len: ${#NEW_NODE_KEY}"
echo "CLIENT_CRT len: ${#NEW_CLIENT_CRT}"
echo "CLIENT_KEY len: ${#NEW_CLIENT_KEY}"
input.description: Extract new certs from toolbox as base64

content.content:
NODE_CRT len: 2220
NODE_KEY len: 2272
CLIENT_CRT len: 1520
CLIENT_KEY len: 2272

tool_use_result.stdout:
NODE_CRT len: 2220
NODE_KEY len: 2272
CLIENT_CRT len: 1520
CLIENT_KEY len: 2272

input.command:
NEW_NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | base64 -w 0)
NEW_NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new | base64 -w 0)
NEW_CLIENT_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new | base64 -w 0)
NEW_CLIENT_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new | base64 -w 0)

kubectl -n cockroachdb patch secret crdb-cluster-certs --type='json' -p="[
  {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/data/node.crt\", \"value\": \"${NEW_NODE_CRT}\"},
  {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/data/node.key\", \"value\": \"${NEW_NODE_KEY}\"},
  {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/data/client.root.crt\", \"value\": \"${NEW_CLIENT_CRT}\"},
  {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/data/client.root.key\", \"value\": \"${NEW_CLIENT_KEY}\"}
]" 2>&1
input.description: Patch secret with new node and client certs

content.content: secret/crdb-cluster-certs patched

content.text: Secret updated. Now rolling restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new certs:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset crdb-cluster 2>&1

content.content: statefulset.apps/crdb-cluster restarted

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset crdb-cluster --timeout=180s 2>&1
input.description: Wait for rolling restart to complete
